instruction: How do I create a proper CMake install configuration so other projects can use find_package() to consume my C++ library?
output:
<think>
Creating a find_package()-compatible CMake library requires several pieces: (1) install targets for headers, libraries, and CMake config files, (2) a *Config.cmake or *-config.cmake file that defines imported targets, (3) a *ConfigVersion.cmake for version compatibility checking, (4) proper namespace for exported targets. The modern approach uses CMakePackageConfigHelpers to generate these files. The key workflow: define your library target, set its public headers, configure install destinations, generate and install the config files. The tricky parts are: setting up the include directory correctly for both build and install trees (using generator expressions), handling transitive dependencies, and supporting both static and shared builds. I should also show how to generate a pkg-config .pc file for non-CMake consumers.
</think>

```cmake
# CMakeLists.txt — Library with proper install/find_package support
cmake_minimum_required(VERSION 3.25)
project(FastJson VERSION 2.1.0 LANGUAGES CXX)

set(CMAKE_CXX_STANDARD 20)
set(CMAKE_CXX_STANDARD_REQUIRED ON)

option(BUILD_SHARED_LIBS "Build shared libraries" ON)
option(FASTJSON_BUILD_TESTS "Build tests" ON)

# Main library target
add_library(fastjson
    src/parser.cpp
    src/serializer.cpp
    src/dom.cpp
    src/simd_parser.cpp
)
add_library(FastJson::fastjson ALIAS fastjson)

# Public headers — different paths for build vs install
target_include_directories(fastjson
    PUBLIC
        $<BUILD_INTERFACE:${CMAKE_CURRENT_SOURCE_DIR}/include>
        $<INSTALL_INTERFACE:${CMAKE_INSTALL_INCLUDEDIR}>
    PRIVATE
        ${CMAKE_CURRENT_SOURCE_DIR}/src
)

# Set library properties
set_target_properties(fastjson PROPERTIES
    VERSION ${PROJECT_VERSION}
    SOVERSION ${PROJECT_VERSION_MAJOR}
    EXPORT_NAME fastjson
    PUBLIC_HEADER ""
)

# Find transitive dependencies — consumers will need these too
find_package(ZLIB REQUIRED)
target_link_libraries(fastjson PUBLIC ZLIB::ZLIB)

# === Installation ===
include(GNUInstallDirs)
include(CMakePackageConfigHelpers)

# Install the library binary
install(TARGETS fastjson
    EXPORT FastJsonTargets
    LIBRARY DESTINATION ${CMAKE_INSTALL_LIBDIR}
    ARCHIVE DESTINATION ${CMAKE_INSTALL_LIBDIR}
    RUNTIME DESTINATION ${CMAKE_INSTALL_BINDIR}
    INCLUDES DESTINATION ${CMAKE_INSTALL_INCLUDEDIR}
)

# Install headers preserving directory structure
install(DIRECTORY include/fastjson
    DESTINATION ${CMAKE_INSTALL_INCLUDEDIR}
    FILES_MATCHING PATTERN "*.hpp" PATTERN "*.h"
)

# Generate and install export targets
install(EXPORT FastJsonTargets
    FILE FastJsonTargets.cmake
    NAMESPACE FastJson::
    DESTINATION ${CMAKE_INSTALL_LIBDIR}/cmake/FastJson
)

# Generate version compatibility file
write_basic_package_version_file(
    "${CMAKE_CURRENT_BINARY_DIR}/FastJsonConfigVersion.cmake"
    VERSION ${PROJECT_VERSION}
    COMPATIBILITY SameMajorVersion
)

# Generate config file from template
configure_package_config_file(
    "${CMAKE_CURRENT_SOURCE_DIR}/cmake/FastJsonConfig.cmake.in"
    "${CMAKE_CURRENT_BINARY_DIR}/FastJsonConfig.cmake"
    INSTALL_DESTINATION ${CMAKE_INSTALL_LIBDIR}/cmake/FastJson
)

# Install cmake config files
install(FILES
    "${CMAKE_CURRENT_BINARY_DIR}/FastJsonConfig.cmake"
    "${CMAKE_CURRENT_BINARY_DIR}/FastJsonConfigVersion.cmake"
    DESTINATION ${CMAKE_INSTALL_LIBDIR}/cmake/FastJson
)
```

```cmake
# cmake/FastJsonConfig.cmake.in — template for find_package
@PACKAGE_INIT@

# Find transitive dependencies
include(CMakeFindDependencyMacro)
find_dependency(ZLIB)

# Import targets
include("${CMAKE_CURRENT_LIST_DIR}/FastJsonTargets.cmake")

# Verify all required components exist
check_required_components(FastJson)
```

```cmake
# Consumer project — uses find_package
cmake_minimum_required(VERSION 3.25)
project(MyApp)

find_package(FastJson 2.0 REQUIRED)  # Version 2.x compatible

add_executable(myapp main.cpp)
target_link_libraries(myapp PRIVATE FastJson::fastjson)
# Automatically gets: include dirs, link libraries, transitive deps (ZLIB)
```

```cpp
// include/fastjson/parser.hpp
#pragma once
#include <string_view>
#include <memory>
#include <expected>

namespace fastjson {

class Document;

enum class ParseError {
    InvalidSyntax,
    UnexpectedEOF,
    InvalidEncoding,
    NumberOverflow,
};

class Parser {
public:
    Parser();
    ~Parser();
    
    std::expected<Document, ParseError> parse(std::string_view json);
    
private:
    struct Impl;
    std::unique_ptr<Impl> impl_;
};

} // namespace fastjson
```

```bash
# Build and install
cmake -B build -DCMAKE_INSTALL_PREFIX=/usr/local
cmake --build build
cmake --install build

# Consumer can now do:
cmake -B build  # find_package(FastJson) works via /usr/local/lib/cmake/FastJson/
```

instruction: Show how to create a CPack configuration for generating DEB, RPM, and Windows installer packages from a CMake project.
output:
<think>
CPack is CMake's packaging tool that generates platform-specific installers from install targets. The key: CPack uses the same `install()` commands as regular CMake, so if your library installs correctly with `cmake --install`, CPack can package it. Configuration is via CPackConfig.cmake or `set(CPACK_*)` variables before `include(CPack)`. For multi-format packaging: DEB needs maintainer info, dependencies, sections; RPM needs spec-like metadata; Windows NSIS/WiX needs registry entries and shortcuts. The main challenge is specifying runtime dependencies correctly per platform — DEB uses `CPACK_DEBIAN_PACKAGE_DEPENDS`, RPM uses `CPACK_RPM_PACKAGE_REQUIRES`, and they have different naming conventions. I should also show component-based packaging where headers go in a -dev/-devel package and runtime libraries in the main package.
</think>

```cmake
# CMakeLists.txt — CPack configuration at end of file
# ... (after all install() commands) ...

# === CPack Configuration ===
set(CPACK_PACKAGE_NAME "fastjson")
set(CPACK_PACKAGE_VERSION ${PROJECT_VERSION})
set(CPACK_PACKAGE_VENDOR "FastJson Project")
set(CPACK_PACKAGE_DESCRIPTION_SUMMARY "High-performance JSON parser with SIMD")
set(CPACK_PACKAGE_HOMEPAGE_URL "https://github.com/example/fastjson")
set(CPACK_PACKAGE_CONTACT "dev@fastjson.example.com")
set(CPACK_RESOURCE_FILE_LICENSE "${CMAKE_CURRENT_SOURCE_DIR}/LICENSE")
set(CPACK_RESOURCE_FILE_README "${CMAKE_CURRENT_SOURCE_DIR}/README.md")

# Component-based packaging: runtime vs development
set(CPACK_COMPONENTS_ALL runtime development)
set(CPACK_COMPONENT_RUNTIME_DISPLAY_NAME "Runtime Libraries")
set(CPACK_COMPONENT_DEVELOPMENT_DISPLAY_NAME "Development Files")
set(CPACK_COMPONENT_DEVELOPMENT_DEPENDS runtime)

# Install targets into components
install(TARGETS fastjson
    EXPORT FastJsonTargets
    LIBRARY DESTINATION ${CMAKE_INSTALL_LIBDIR} COMPONENT runtime
    ARCHIVE DESTINATION ${CMAKE_INSTALL_LIBDIR} COMPONENT development
    RUNTIME DESTINATION ${CMAKE_INSTALL_BINDIR} COMPONENT runtime
)
install(DIRECTORY include/fastjson
    DESTINATION ${CMAKE_INSTALL_INCLUDEDIR}
    COMPONENT development
)

# === DEB-specific ===
set(CPACK_DEBIAN_PACKAGE_MAINTAINER "FastJson Dev <dev@fastjson.example.com>")
set(CPACK_DEBIAN_PACKAGE_SECTION "libs")
set(CPACK_DEBIAN_PACKAGE_DEPENDS "zlib1g (>= 1.2.11)")
set(CPACK_DEBIAN_PACKAGE_SHLIBDEPS ON)  # Auto-detect shared lib deps
set(CPACK_DEB_COMPONENT_INSTALL ON)
set(CPACK_DEBIAN_RUNTIME_PACKAGE_NAME "libfastjson2")
set(CPACK_DEBIAN_DEVELOPMENT_PACKAGE_NAME "libfastjson-dev")
set(CPACK_DEBIAN_DEVELOPMENT_PACKAGE_DEPENDS "libfastjson2 (= ${CPACK_PACKAGE_VERSION})")

# === RPM-specific ===
set(CPACK_RPM_PACKAGE_LICENSE "MIT")
set(CPACK_RPM_PACKAGE_GROUP "Development/Libraries")
set(CPACK_RPM_PACKAGE_REQUIRES "zlib >= 1.2.11")
set(CPACK_RPM_COMPONENT_INSTALL ON)
set(CPACK_RPM_RUNTIME_PACKAGE_NAME "fastjson-libs")
set(CPACK_RPM_DEVELOPMENT_PACKAGE_NAME "fastjson-devel")
set(CPACK_RPM_DEVELOPMENT_PACKAGE_REQUIRES "fastjson-libs = ${CPACK_PACKAGE_VERSION}")

# === Windows NSIS ===
set(CPACK_NSIS_DISPLAY_NAME "FastJson ${PROJECT_VERSION}")
set(CPACK_NSIS_PACKAGE_NAME "FastJson")
set(CPACK_NSIS_MODIFY_PATH ON)  # Add to PATH
set(CPACK_NSIS_ENABLE_UNINSTALL_BEFORE_INSTALL ON)

include(CPack)
```

```bash
#!/bin/bash
# scripts/build_packages.sh — Build all package types
set -euo pipefail

VERSION=$(cmake -P -D "print_version=1" CMakeLists.txt 2>&1 || echo "2.1.0")
echo "Building packages for FastJson $VERSION"

# Build release
cmake -B build -DCMAKE_BUILD_TYPE=Release -DCMAKE_INSTALL_PREFIX=/usr
cmake --build build --config Release -j$(nproc)

# Generate DEB packages
cd build
cpack -G DEB
# Produces: fastjson-2.1.0-Linux-runtime.deb, fastjson-2.1.0-Linux-development.deb

# Generate RPM packages
cpack -G RPM
# Produces: fastjson-libs-2.1.0.rpm, fastjson-devel-2.1.0.rpm

# Generate tarball
cpack -G TGZ
# Produces: fastjson-2.1.0-Linux.tar.gz

# Verify DEB package
dpkg-deb --info fastjson-2.1.0-Linux-runtime.deb
dpkg-deb --contents fastjson-2.1.0-Linux-runtime.deb

# Verify RPM package
rpm -qip fastjson-libs-2.1.0.rpm
rpm -qlp fastjson-libs-2.1.0.rpm
```

```yaml
# .github/workflows/packages.yml — CI for multi-platform packages
name: Build Packages
on:
  push:
    tags: ['v*']

jobs:
  linux-packages:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
      - name: Install build deps
        run: sudo apt-get install -y zlib1g-dev rpm
      - name: Build
        run: |
          cmake -B build -DCMAKE_BUILD_TYPE=Release -DCMAKE_INSTALL_PREFIX=/usr
          cmake --build build -j$(nproc)
      - name: Package DEB
        run: cd build && cpack -G DEB
      - name: Package RPM
        run: cd build && cpack -G RPM
      - uses: actions/upload-artifact@v4
        with:
          name: linux-packages
          path: build/*.deb build/*.rpm

  windows-package:
    runs-on: windows-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
      - name: Build
        run: |
          cmake -B build -DCMAKE_BUILD_TYPE=Release
          cmake --build build --config Release
      - name: Package NSIS
        run: cd build && cpack -G NSIS
      - uses: actions/upload-artifact@v4
        with:
          name: windows-installer
          path: build/*.exe
```

instruction: How do I write a proper CMake find module (FindMyLib.cmake) for a third-party library that doesn't provide CMake config files, including pkg-config fallback?
output:
<think>
Writing a FindXxx.cmake module is needed when a library provides no CMake support. The module searches standard paths, uses pkg-config as a fallback, and creates IMPORTED targets that behave like modern CMake targets. The key components: (1) find_path for headers, (2) find_library for the library file, (3) pkg_check_modules as a fallback, (4) FindPackageHandleStandardArgs for the standard FOUND/REQUIRED/QUIET handling, (5) creating an IMPORTED target so consumers use target_link_libraries. The tricky parts: handling different library names on different platforms (e.g., libfoo.so vs foo.lib), supporting both static and shared variants, version detection from headers, and setting the right IMPORTED target properties. I should also show how to handle multi-component find modules (like FindBoost finding individual components).
</think>

```cmake
# cmake/FindLevelDB.cmake — Find module for LevelDB
#
# Finds the LevelDB library.
#
# Imported Targets:
#   LevelDB::LevelDB     - The LevelDB library
#
# Result Variables:
#   LevelDB_FOUND        - True if LevelDB was found
#   LevelDB_INCLUDE_DIRS - Include directories
#   LevelDB_LIBRARIES    - Libraries to link
#   LevelDB_VERSION      - Version string

# First, try pkg-config (most reliable on Linux)
find_package(PkgConfig QUIET)
if(PKG_CONFIG_FOUND)
    pkg_check_modules(PC_LevelDB QUIET leveldb)
endif()

# Find header — use pkg-config hints if available
find_path(LevelDB_INCLUDE_DIR
    NAMES leveldb/db.h
    PATHS
        ${PC_LevelDB_INCLUDE_DIRS}
        /usr/local/include
        /usr/include
        /opt/homebrew/include  # macOS ARM
    PATH_SUFFIXES include
)

# Find library
find_library(LevelDB_LIBRARY
    NAMES leveldb libleveldb
    PATHS
        ${PC_LevelDB_LIBRARY_DIRS}
        /usr/local/lib
        /usr/lib
        /usr/lib/x86_64-linux-gnu
        /opt/homebrew/lib
    PATH_SUFFIXES lib lib64
)

# Extract version from header
if(LevelDB_INCLUDE_DIR AND EXISTS "${LevelDB_INCLUDE_DIR}/leveldb/db.h")
    file(STRINGS "${LevelDB_INCLUDE_DIR}/leveldb/db.h" _version_lines
        REGEX "#define LEVELDB_(MAJOR|MINOR|PATCH)_VERSION")

    foreach(_line ${_version_lines})
        if(_line MATCHES "MAJOR_VERSION +([0-9]+)")
            set(_major ${CMAKE_MATCH_1})
        elseif(_line MATCHES "MINOR_VERSION +([0-9]+)")
            set(_minor ${CMAKE_MATCH_1})
        elseif(_line MATCHES "PATCH_VERSION +([0-9]+)")
            set(_patch ${CMAKE_MATCH_1})
        endif()
    endforeach()

    if(DEFINED _major)
        set(LevelDB_VERSION "${_major}.${_minor}.${_patch}")
    elseif(PC_LevelDB_VERSION)
        set(LevelDB_VERSION ${PC_LevelDB_VERSION})
    endif()
endif()

# Standard find_package result handling
include(FindPackageHandleStandardArgs)
find_package_handle_standard_args(LevelDB
    REQUIRED_VARS LevelDB_LIBRARY LevelDB_INCLUDE_DIR
    VERSION_VAR LevelDB_VERSION
)

# Create imported target
if(LevelDB_FOUND AND NOT TARGET LevelDB::LevelDB)
    add_library(LevelDB::LevelDB UNKNOWN IMPORTED)
    set_target_properties(LevelDB::LevelDB PROPERTIES
        IMPORTED_LOCATION "${LevelDB_LIBRARY}"
        INTERFACE_INCLUDE_DIRECTORIES "${LevelDB_INCLUDE_DIR}"
    )

    # Add pkg-config compile flags if available
    if(PC_LevelDB_CFLAGS_OTHER)
        set_target_properties(LevelDB::LevelDB PROPERTIES
            INTERFACE_COMPILE_OPTIONS "${PC_LevelDB_CFLAGS_OTHER}"
        )
    endif()

    # LevelDB depends on pthreads on Linux
    if(UNIX AND NOT APPLE)
        find_package(Threads REQUIRED)
        set_property(TARGET LevelDB::LevelDB APPEND PROPERTY
            INTERFACE_LINK_LIBRARIES Threads::Threads)
    endif()
endif()

mark_as_advanced(LevelDB_INCLUDE_DIR LevelDB_LIBRARY)

# Set legacy variables
set(LevelDB_INCLUDE_DIRS ${LevelDB_INCLUDE_DIR})
set(LevelDB_LIBRARIES ${LevelDB_LIBRARY})
```

```cmake
# Consumer usage:
list(APPEND CMAKE_MODULE_PATH "${CMAKE_SOURCE_DIR}/cmake")
find_package(LevelDB 1.23 REQUIRED)

add_executable(myapp main.cpp)
target_link_libraries(myapp PRIVATE LevelDB::LevelDB)
```

```cpp
// main.cpp — using LevelDB found by our module
#include <leveldb/db.h>
#include <iostream>

int main() {
    leveldb::DB* db;
    leveldb::Options options;
    options.create_if_missing = true;

    leveldb::Status status = leveldb::DB::Open(options, "/tmp/testdb", &db);
    if (!status.ok()) {
        std::cerr << "Failed to open DB: " << status.ToString() << std::endl;
        return 1;
    }

    db->Put(leveldb::WriteOptions(), "key", "value");

    std::string value;
    db->Get(leveldb::ReadOptions(), "key", &value);
    std::cout << "Read: " << value << std::endl;

    delete db;
    return 0;
}
```

instruction: Show how to generate and install a pkg-config .pc file from a CMake project for consumption by non-CMake build systems.
output:
<think>
pkg-config is the universal C/C++ dependency discovery mechanism outside of CMake. Many projects (Meson, autotools, plain Makefiles) use `pkg-config --cflags --libs` to find dependencies. CMake can generate .pc files using configure_file() with a .pc.in template. The key fields in a .pc file: prefix, libdir, includedir, Name, Description, Version, Requires (other pkg-config deps), Libs (linker flags), Cflags (compiler flags). The tricky part is handling the prefix relocation — .pc files use a prefix variable that gets substituted at install time. I should also handle private dependencies (Requires.private, Libs.private) which are needed for static linking but not shared. The Libs field should use -L and -l flags, not absolute paths, for portability.
</think>

```cmake
# CMakeLists.txt — pkg-config generation
cmake_minimum_required(VERSION 3.25)
project(FastJson VERSION 2.1.0 LANGUAGES CXX)

include(GNUInstallDirs)

add_library(fastjson src/parser.cpp src/serializer.cpp)
target_include_directories(fastjson PUBLIC
    $<BUILD_INTERFACE:${CMAKE_CURRENT_SOURCE_DIR}/include>
    $<INSTALL_INTERFACE:${CMAKE_INSTALL_INCLUDEDIR}>
)

find_package(ZLIB REQUIRED)
target_link_libraries(fastjson PUBLIC ZLIB::ZLIB)

# Generate pkg-config file from template
configure_file(
    "${CMAKE_CURRENT_SOURCE_DIR}/cmake/fastjson.pc.in"
    "${CMAKE_CURRENT_BINARY_DIR}/fastjson.pc"
    @ONLY
)

# Install the .pc file
install(FILES "${CMAKE_CURRENT_BINARY_DIR}/fastjson.pc"
    DESTINATION "${CMAKE_INSTALL_LIBDIR}/pkgconfig"
)

# Also install library and headers (as before)
install(TARGETS fastjson
    LIBRARY DESTINATION ${CMAKE_INSTALL_LIBDIR}
    ARCHIVE DESTINATION ${CMAKE_INSTALL_LIBDIR}
)
install(DIRECTORY include/fastjson
    DESTINATION ${CMAKE_INSTALL_INCLUDEDIR}
)
```

```
# cmake/fastjson.pc.in — pkg-config template
prefix=@CMAKE_INSTALL_PREFIX@
exec_prefix=${prefix}
libdir=${prefix}/@CMAKE_INSTALL_LIBDIR@
includedir=${prefix}/@CMAKE_INSTALL_INCLUDEDIR@

Name: FastJson
Description: High-performance JSON parser with SIMD acceleration
Version: @PROJECT_VERSION@
URL: https://github.com/example/fastjson

Requires: zlib
Libs: -L${libdir} -lfastjson
Libs.private: -lpthread
Cflags: -I${includedir}
```

```makefile
# Consumer: Makefile using pkg-config
CXX = g++
CXXFLAGS = -std=c++20 -Wall $(shell pkg-config --cflags fastjson)
LDFLAGS = $(shell pkg-config --libs fastjson)

myapp: main.cpp
	$(CXX) $(CXXFLAGS) $< -o $@ $(LDFLAGS)

# Static linking — uses Libs.private and Requires.private
myapp-static: main.cpp
	$(CXX) $(CXXFLAGS) $< -o $@ $(shell pkg-config --libs --static fastjson)

.PHONY: check-deps
check-deps:
	@pkg-config --exists fastjson && echo "FastJson found" || echo "FastJson not found"
	@pkg-config --modversion fastjson
```

```python
# Meson consumer: meson.build
project('myapp', 'cpp', version: '1.0', default_options: ['cpp_std=c++20'])

fastjson_dep = dependency('fastjson', version: '>=2.0', method: 'pkg-config')

executable('myapp', 'main.cpp', dependencies: [fastjson_dep])
```

```bash
# Verify pkg-config output
pkg-config --cflags fastjson
# -I/usr/local/include

pkg-config --libs fastjson
# -L/usr/local/lib -lfastjson -lz

pkg-config --libs --static fastjson
# -L/usr/local/lib -lfastjson -lz -lpthread

pkg-config --modversion fastjson
# 2.1.0

# Custom prefix (for non-standard install locations)
PKG_CONFIG_PATH=/opt/fastjson/lib/pkgconfig pkg-config --cflags fastjson
```
